explanations.8: This maps port 11434 from inside the container to port 11434 on your computer, so you can talk to the ollama service from the outside.
explanations.10: This creates a named storage area called ollama_data that persists even if the container stops, and mounts it to /root/.ollama inside the container.
explanations.25: This maps port 4000 on your computer to port 8080 inside the container, so the web interface is accessible at localhost:4000.
explanations.27: This allows the open-webui container to reach your host machine using the special hostname host.docker.internal, which Docker automatically translates.
explanations.29: This tells open-webui where to find the ollama service by using the container name 'ollama' as a hostname, since Docker networks let containers talk to each other this way.
explanations.34: This ensures ollama starts before open-webui, since open-webui needs ollama to be running first to function properly.
